results.content: Through advanced testing- genomics, biological age mapping, and real-time biometrics- longevity medicine helps you see your body’s blueprint. This year’s Summit is dedicated to leading researchers sharing insights across key areas of longevity science — from dementia and hormone health to air quality, sleep, and cellular reprogramming. ⸻ What Longevity Really Is : True longevity medicine is not about chasing immortality - it’s about preserving human function as long as possible. In this feature, I explore: ·      Why the current healthcare model is failing to address root causes ·      How symptoms are actually signals that guide us back to physiological balance ·      The science and philosophy behind the Levitas 8R Longevity Framework ·      The importance of cellular health, mitochondrial optimisation, metabolic repair, and nervous-system regulation ·      How removing interference — toxins, stress, emotional load — activates the body’s innate healing intelligence Longevity is more than extending lifespan. For policymakers, city planners and health leaders, cleaner air isn’t optional, it’s a public health strategy for cognitive longevity.

  What if aging wasn’t something that had to happen to you- but something you could measure, manage, and even slow down? Longevity medicine is changing what it means to grow older.   As fellow Co-Founder of [Unlimited Life](https://www.linkedin.com/company/unlimited-life-movement?trk=public_post-text) Dr. Timothy Cook, MD, FRCPC, MPH, LCol (Ret'd), CD- one of the world’s foremost longevity experts- explains, we can now understand why you’re aging, and begin to turn off that process to help you stay biologically younger, longer.   It’s not science fiction. It’s performance management for your biology. Through advanced testing- genomics, biological age mapping, and real-time biometrics- longevity medicine helps you see your body’s blueprint. And when you can measure it, you can improve it.   Imagine optimizing not just your lifespan, but your healthspan- the number of years you live with vitality, clarity, and purpose.   That’s what [Unlimited Life](https://www.linkedin.com/company/unlimited-life-movement?trk=public_post-text) is about: using cutting-edge science to expand the human experience- not just to live longer, but to live healthier, and fulfilled.   🧬 Can our microbiome shape how we feel? A new meta-analysis in npj Biofilms and Microbiomes by Kowal et al. (2025) provides the first systematic evidence linking microbiome diversity with psychological well-being. The findings are striking: individuals with a richer, more diverse microbiome tend to report higher life satisfaction and better emotional health. Notably, the bacterial phylum Bacillota showed a particularly strong association with well-being. At MusB Research, we’re passionate about uncovering the hidden microbial communities in our gut and exploring the secrets they hold in blood and urine — from physical health markers to mental resilience and overall wellness. We welcome collaborations!   The Science Summit at Oxford this morning opened with remarks from Jim Mellon, Co-Founder of The Longevity Forum, and Lord Neil Mendoza, Provost of Oriel College.   🧬 The Biggest Misconception in Longevity Medicine is…….. When people hear longevity, they imagine cryotherapy chambers, IV drips, and biomarker dashboards lighting up like stock tickers. But that’s not longevity medicine. ⸻ What Longevity Really Is : True longevity medicine is not about chasing immortality - it’s about preserving human function as long as possible. It’s not about living forever. It’s about living fully. And yet, the biggest misconception persists: that longevity is about adding - more supplements, more devices, more data. In reality, the most powerful interventions are about subtracting: noise, inconsistency, inflammation, decision fatigue. The biology of longevity thrives on stability, not stimulation. ⸻ The Myth of the “Data-Driven Savior” We love numbers because they give us a sense of control. But no blood test can fix a lifestyle built on chaos. No biomarker can compensate for poor sleep, chronic stress, and isolation. Data doesn’t change your life — discipline does. The lab report is a mirror, not a solution. ⸻ The Real Longevity Stack Forget protocols that read like startup pitch decks. Here’s what science still can’t out-engineer: • Sleep: the most potent anti-aging therapy we have. • Movement: strength training is neuroprotective, not aesthetic. • Nutrition: mitochondria don’t understand trends; they understand consistency. • Stress: longevity collapses when cortisol rules the system. • Connection: people who age well, don’t age alone. You can’t outsource these. You can only build them. ⸻ The Shift That’s Coming Longevity isn’t a new branch of medicine. It’s the integration of everything we already know biology, psychology, technology, and behavior - into one unified system of preventive precision. The future isn’t in chasing exotic biomarkers. It’s in teaching behavior to catch up with biology. ⸻ A Thought If you knew your biological age today would it change how you live tomorrow? Because the real goal isn’t to extend lifespan. It’s to make every year you already have fuller and more functional.   Clean air is brain care. A recent review published in the National Library of Medicine reveals compelling evidence that long-term exposure to fine particulate matter (PM2.5) and nitrogen dioxide (NO2) significantly increases dementia risk. The mechanisms are clear; oxidative stress, neuroinflammation and vascular injury, all leading to accelerated cognitive decline. What we breathe today could shape how our brains age tomorrow. This shifts the narrative: air quality isn’t just an environmental issue, it’s a neuroprotective priority. For policymakers, city planners and health leaders, cleaner air isn’t optional, it’s a public health strategy for cognitive longevity. 🌟 Highlights from the quantum innovation lab 2025 – Bootcamp 🌟
A few weeks ago, we wrapped up this year’s quantum innovation lab (qilab) Bootcamp of four days and three nights in Bad Ischl
This year, we brought together 24 quantum and physics researchers from across these institutes to Bad Ischl for an intensive entrepreneurship bootcamp, learning about:
🧩 What does it take to become an entrepreneur?
💡 idea validation & value proposition
🚀 deep-tech innovation & technology readiness
🧠 IP strategy & pitch training
…with just a sprinkle of well-deserved breaks in between!
Thanks to our brilliant mentors and speakers, who made the experience unforgettable: [Alexander Schwartz](https://at.linkedin.com/in/alexander-schwartz-4462a7?trk=public_post-text), [Anna Bandura](https://at.linkedin.com/in/anna-bandura?trk=public_post-text), [Arushi Jain](https://at.linkedin.com/in/arushijain-1618?trk=public_post-text), [Christopher Trummer](https://de.linkedin.com/in/christopher-trummer-a58695108?trk=public_post-text), [Clara Conrad-Billroth](https://at.linkedin.com/in/clara-conrad-billroth?trk=public_post-text), Claudia K, [Dadhichi Paretkar](https://de.linkedin.com/in/dadhichiparetkar?trk=public_post-text), [Edmundo R. Sánchez Guajardo](https://at.linkedin.com/in/ersg?trk=public_post-text), [Fraser Levick](https://ch.linkedin.com/in/fraser-levick-900040a0?trk=public_post-text), [Helene Hainzer](https://at.linkedin.com/in/helene-hainzer-b726631a7?trk=public_post-text), [Holger Bock](https://at.linkedin.com/in/holgerbock?trk=public_post-text), [Marco Masia](https://at.linkedin.com/in/mmasia?trk=public_post-text) & [Nicola Weiroster](https://nl.linkedin.com/in/nicola-weiroster?trk=public_post-text)
Special thanks to: [Nir Minerbi](https://il.linkedin.com/in/nir-minerbi?trk=public_post-text) ([Classiq](https://il.linkedin.com/company/classiq-technologies?trk=public_post-text)) and [Tommaso Macrì](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tommaso-macri-quantum?trk=public_post-text) ([QuEra Computing Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/quera-computing-inc?trk=public_post-text))
After being intensely grilled for four days, our teams are now continuing their journey through expert-led webinars on funding, financial planning, market analysis, and more, guided by [Julia Wagner](https://ch.linkedin.com/in/julia-wagner-austria?trk=public_post-text), [Maximilian Konrad](https://de.linkedin.com/in/maximilian-konrad?trk=public_post-text), [Matthias C. Kettemann](https://at.linkedin.com/in/matthias-c-kettemann-51b582215?trk=public_post-text), [Tobias Denzler](https://ch.linkedin.com/in/tdenzler/en?trk=public_post-text), [Markus Pietzka](https://at.linkedin.com/in/markuspietzka?trk=public_post-text), [James S.](https://ch.linkedin.com/in/jsanders123?trk=public_post-text) and [Carlos Fernández de Retana](https://at.linkedin.com/in/carlos-fern%C3%A1ndez-de-retana-31555a12?trk=public_post-text)
🎯 What’s next?
Stay tuned, the finale is coming soon!
On 14 November, the teams will pitch their ideas in front of peers and jury members at the qilab Demo Day in Vienna.
We would like to thank our partners: [accent Inkubator GmbH](https://at.linkedin.com/company/accent-inkubator-gmbh?trk=public_post-text), [INiTS | Vienna's High-Tech Incubator](https://at.linkedin.com/company/initsvienna?trk=public_post-text), [Onsight Ventures](https://at.linkedin.com/company/onsight-ventures?trk=public_post-text), [Positron Ventures](https://nl.linkedin.com/company/positron-ventures?trk=public_post-text), [QAI Ventures](https://ch.linkedin.com/company/qaiventures?trk=public_post-text), [Quantum Science Austria](https://at.linkedin.com/company/quantum-science-austria?trk=public_post-text), [TUM Venture Labs](https://de.linkedin.com/company/tum-venture-labs?trk=public_post-text), [univie innovation](https://at.linkedin.com/company/univie-innovation?trk=public_post-text), [VCQ Vienna Center for Quantum Science and Technology](https://at.linkedin.com/company/vcq-vienna-center-for-quantum-science-and-technology?trk=public_post-text), [xista science ventures](https://at.linkedin.com/company/xsv?trk=public_post-text), [Wirtschaftsagentur Wien](https://at.linkedin.com/company/wirtschaftsagenturwien?trk=public_post-text), [QDNL Participations](https://nl.linkedin.com/company/qdnl-participations?trk=public_post-text), [Gateway Excellence Start-up Center University of Cologne](https://de.linkedin.com/school/gatewayesc/?trk=public_post-text)

🚀 Day 2 of the Chicago Quantum Summit 2025 was a masterclass in momentum — from federal funding to startup breakthroughs, and now, qreal-world quantum impact.
🎙️ I had the privilege of hearing from:
🔹 [Darío Gil](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dar%C3%ADo-gil-58575713?trk=public_post-text) (DOE) — announcing renewed funding for 5 National Quantum Centers, including Q-NEXT & SQMS
🔹 Troy Campbell (Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago) — on quantum’s role in reshaping finance & risk modeling
🔹[Mung Chiang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mung-chiang-9511445?trk=public_post-text) (Purdue) & [Chetan Nayak](https://www.linkedin.com/in/cnayak-quantum?trk=public_post-text) [Microsoft](https://www.linkedin.com/company/microsoft?trk=public_post-text) — on the Midwest’s booming quantum startup corridor
🔹 Terry Rudolph [PsiQuantum](https://www.linkedin.com/company/psiquantum?trk=public_post-text) — on quantum-generated data unlocking new AI frontiers
🔹 [Lorenzo Martinelli](https://www.linkedin.com/in/martinelli-lorenzo?trk=public_post-text) [D-Wave](https://ca.linkedin.com/company/d-wave-quantum?trk=public_post-text) — spotlighting how annealing-based quantum computers are already solving complex optimization problems that stump classical systems.
💡 The entrepreneurial energy was palpable: 🌍 At the IQMP Growing Global Startups Workshop, I learned how international founders — even on visas — can launch quantum ventures in the U.S.
🌱 And I’m proud to be part of the Alchemist Chicago cohort — a powerful collaboration between the Polsky Center and [Alchemist Accelerator](https://www.linkedin.com/company/alchemist-accelerator?trk=public_post-text), uniting 27 deep tech startups across AI, biotech, and quantum.
This is more than a summit — it’s a movement.
We’re not just talking about the future of quantum — we’re building it. 🛠️
Let’s connect and collaborate:

Let's talk about what happens when science stops chasing youth and starts quantifying it. [Generation Lab](https://www.linkedin.com/company/generation-lab-longevity?trk=public_post-text) just raised $11M in seed funding, led by [Accel](https://www.linkedin.com/company/accel-vc?trk=public_post-text), marking Accel's 1st-ever longevity bet. Not a small move. Accel doesn't throw darts at startup boards. They play chess, and they just made a bold opening move into the biotech future of staying younger, longer.
Founded by [Alina Rui Su](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alinasu?trk=public_post-text), Dr. Irina Conboy, and [Michael Suswal](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-suswal-51b45421?trk=public_post-text), Generation Lab isn't just talking about [#aging](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Faging&trk=public_post-text); they're measuring it at the molecular level. Alina Rui Su, [Harvard Medical School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-medical-school/?trk=public_post-text) PhD, [University of California, Berkeley](https://www.linkedin.com/school/uc-berkeley/?trk=public_post-text) bioengineering alum, [Forbes](https://www.linkedin.com/company/forbes-magazine?trk=public_post-text) 30 Under 30, and someone who filed her 1st medical patent at 15, teamed up with Dr. Irina Conboy, the UC Berkeley professor whose [#research](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fresearch&trk=public_post-text) on plasma dilution flipped [#agingbiology](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fagingbiology&trk=public_post-text) on its head. Add Michael Suswal, co-founder of [Standard AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/standard-ai?trk=public_post-text) and a man who knows how to scale systems from lab bench to market trench, and you've got a team built for impact.
Their platform, SystemAge™, doesn't guess your [#biologicalage](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbiologicalage&trk=public_post-text), it measures it. With over 460 [#epigenetic](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fepigenetic&trk=public_post-text) markers across 19 organ systems, Generation Lab can tell you how old your [#brain](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrain&trk=public_post-text), [#heart](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fheart&trk=public_post-text), [#liver](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fliver&trk=public_post-text), or [#immunesystem](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fimmunesystem&trk=public_post-text) really is. They call it "measuring biological noise." Translation: the closer your body is to static, the younger you are. Clinical validation already shows results like a 13.6-year [#brainage](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrainage&trk=public_post-text) reversal using MUSE [#Stemcell](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fstemcell&trk=public_post-text) treatments. That's not sci-fi. That's data.
This seed round, featuring [Samsung Next](https://www.linkedin.com/company/samsung-next?trk=public_post-text), [Zone2](https://www.linkedin.com/company/z2-zone2?trk=public_post-text), Steve Aoki's [Aoki Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/aokilabs?trk=public_post-text), Giannis Antetokounmpo's [BYL Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/bylventures?trk=public_post-text), and [Simu Liu](https://www.linkedin.com/in/simu-liu-47946a2b1?trk=public_post-text)'s [Markham Valley Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/markhamvalley?trk=public_post-text), pushes total funding to $15M. Big names, sure, but more importantly, diverse minds from entertainment, sports, and tech betting on [#longevity](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flongevity&trk=public_post-text) as the next frontier. When George Church, [Aubrey de Grey](https://www.linkedin.com/in/aubrey-de-grey-24260b?trk=public_post-text), and [Dr. Adeel Khan, MD](https://ae.linkedin.com/in/dradeelkhan-md?trk=public_post-text) are on your scientific advisory board, you're not following the trend; you're building the timeline.
In under 10 months, Generation Lab has expanded to 275+ clinics across 18 countries, aggregating 300M+ data points. Their tech isn't a supplement or a wellness app, it's clinical-grade [#diagnostics](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdiagnostics&trk=public_post-text) built to turn [#healthspan](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhealthspan&trk=public_post-text) into a measurable metric. The company's HQ in SF might be small, but the ambition isn't.
The play here isn't immortality, it's accountability. Knowing how your organs are aging before symptoms show up changes the entire conversation around healthcare. Generation Lab isn't selling forever; they're selling control. That's the kind of disruption Accel just paid attention to.

💜 TSVC 2025 Annual General Meeting
Yesterday, we gathered with our limited partners, founders, and friends for the [#TSVC](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftsvc&trk=public_post-text) 2025 Annual General Meeting, centered on this year’s theme: AI Frontiers & Made-in-USA. 🇺🇸
🎙️The evening explored how the next wave of innovation goes beyond software, moving toward the era of Physical AI, where intelligence meets manufacturing, energy, and materials in the real world.
Seven of our portfolio founders shared insights across diverse frontiers. From programmable optics and real-time AI platforms to personalized medicine for fertility, solid-state batteries, and clean energy solutions, showing how deep-tech innovation is shaping the future of industry.
[Eugene Zhang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/eugenezhang?trk=public_post-text), Founding Partner, opened with a discussion on opportunities in building Physical AI, followed by [Lisheng Wang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lisheng-wang-9796883?trk=public_post-text), General Partner, who presented fund performance highlights. [Spencer Greene](https://www.linkedin.com/in/spencergreene?trk=public_post-text), General Partner, closed with an outlook on how evolving policy and market shifts are guiding venture strategies for the year ahead.
👏 Thank you to all our founders, guests, and partners who joined us for an evening of reflection, conversation, and shared vision.
Founded in 2010 by [Eugene Zhang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/eugenezhang?trk=public_post-text) and [Chun Xia](https://www.linkedin.com/in/chunrayxia?trk=public_post-text), TSVC celebrates 15 years of supporting visionary founders who are building transformative technologies around the world. 🌍
Here’s to another year of bold ideas and lasting impact. 🚀

Coordinated by VTT in collaboration with local and national partners, Finland selected VTT LaunchPad as one of NATO DIANA's accelerator sites, resulting in the establishment of the VTT Dual-Use LaunchPad. Its mission: to accelerate the development of deep tech and dual-use innovations in key strategic areas such as 5G/6G, quantum technologies, and space.

French deeptech startup [Isentroniq](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/isentroniq?trk=public_post-text) has raised €7.5m to tackle quantum computing’s wiring bottleneck, aiming to make million-qubit systems feasible within standard cryostats.
The constraint is physical as much as computational. Each control line adds heat, takes space and costs money inside dilution refrigerators cooled to c. 0.01°C above absolute zero. Isentroniq is building a next-generation superconducting wiring architecture that cuts heat load, footprint and expense, which could unlock orders of magnitude more qubits per system and bring large-scale machines within reach of commercial budgets.
Paris-based and fabless, the company designs the wiring stack and relies on specialised partners for production, combining scientific depth with industrial execution. Co-founders Dr [Paul Magnard](https://fr.linkedin.com/in/paul-magnard-a998b718a?trk=public_post-text), CEO, and [Théodore AMAR](https://fr.linkedin.com/in/th%C3%A9odore-amar-b362155b?trk=public_post-text) bring superconducting qubit expertise and go-to-market focus, and plan to deliver an MVP in 2026 before moving to full commercial roll-out.
⚡️ €7.5m round led by [Heartcore Capital](https://dk.linkedin.com/company/heartcorecapital?trk=public_post-text), with [OVNI Capital](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/ovnicap?trk=public_post-text), [Kima Ventures](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/kima-ventures?trk=public_post-text), [IXCORE GROUP](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/ixcore?trk=public_post-text), [Better Angle](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/better-angle?trk=public_post-text), [Epsilon Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/epsilon-ventures?trk=public_post-text), plus support from [Bpifrance](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/bpifrance?trk=public_post-text) and ANR under France 2030
⚡️ Wiring innovation designed to integrate c. 1,000× more qubits per cryostat
⚡️ Target reduction in million-qubit system cost to c. €50m
⚡️ Fabless model with global manufacturing partners
⚡️ MVP planned for 2026, then pilot integrations and scale-up
A sharp focus on the unglamorous layer of wiring could be exactly what superconducting quantum systems need to move from labs to datacentres. Rooting for the team as they turn infrastructure from blocker to accelerator.

# Introducing Generation Lab on #fortune

![View profile for Alina Rui Su]()

Generation Lab•8K followers

Today marks a significant milestone as we officially launch [Generation Lab](https://www.linkedin.com/company/generation-lab-longevity?trk=public_post-text) on [#fortune](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ffortune&trk=public_post-text). As the cofounder and CEO, I want to share the personal journey that led me to this moment and why I believe so profoundly in our mission to make health accessible for all.
We are running out of time.
Reflecting on the finite nature of our lives, I realized the urgency of my mission. If I were to live until 80, I have only 11,389 days of clear, conscious time left without sleeping or eating. This realization was a stark reminder of the limited time we have to make a meaningful impact.
[Harvard Medical School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-medical-school/?trk=public_post-text) was a dream I’d pursued since I was 7, driven by a passion for contributing to medicine. But outside the lab, I saw a real-world struggle for accessible preventive health. It took me 6 years to convince Irina Conboy , my professor and mentor at [University of California, Berkeley](https://www.linkedin.com/school/uc-berkeley/?trk=public_post-text), where I researched longevity for four years in her lab, that we needed to build a company that truly impacts lives with the knowledge we have. While exiting my last company through [Techstars](https://www.linkedin.com/company/techstars?trk=public_post-text), I met [Michael Suswal](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-suswal-51b45421?trk=public_post-text) , who shared the same vision.
My mission is rooted in the belief that everyone deserves equal access to health and longevity. I see our work as a way to honor the wisdom and achievements of those who came before us and to ensure that the benefits of modern science and technology are accessible to all. I will make every moment count and work towards a future where health is a right, not a privilege.
It is the power source that keeps me going and inspires me to dedicate my remaining days to improving the world. At [Generation Lab](https://www.linkedin.com/company/generation-lab-longevity?trk=public_post-text), we are committed to innovating and providing solutions that extend healthy living for everyone.
Leaving my [Harvard Medical School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-medical-school/?trk=public_post-text) PhD program was not an easy decision, but it was a necessary one. I chose to devote my full energy and focus to this cause. I believe that by leveraging the power of collective intelligence and technology, we can achieve remarkable things.
Generation Lab stands for a future where health and longevity are not privileges but fundamental rights. Together, we can ensure that every individual has the opportunity to live a long, healthy, and fulfilling life.
The 3 of us turned that vision into reality, resulting in the product you hold today. It represents 20 years of aging research, three driven co-founders fighting as their life mission, and an incredible team committed to benefiting humanity.
As I embark on this journey, I am filled with hope and determination. I dedicate my work to the precious time we all have and the impact we can make together. Here’s to making every moment count, for ourselves and for the generations to come.
To life and health,
Alina Su
Cofounder and CEO, Generation Lab

# Generation Lab: Aging is software, not hardware

![View profile for Delphine Le Grand]()

Eudēmonia Summit•28K followers

[Generation Lab](https://www.linkedin.com/company/generation-lab-longevity?trk=public_post-text) was born from a simple truth:
You only get 15,300 days of real life.
That’s what [Alina Rui Su](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alinasu?trk=public_post-text) realized early on, after losing her grandfather before they ever met.
The math is sobering: 37,000 days if you live to 80.
Subtract sleep, work, and the basics, and you're left with just over 15,000 days of real, awake life...
So she asked herself the biggest question: how do we make those days longer, and better?
The answer led to Generation Lab, a company spun out from Convoy Lab after 20 years of aging research.
Here’s what they’re building, and why it matters:
✅ 𝗔𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝘀𝗻’𝘁 𝗳𝗮𝘁𝗲, 𝗶𝘁’𝘀 𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗼𝗽𝘆
Your genes are hardware. But aging? That’s the software, dynamic, reactive, and full of noise. Generation Lab is learning how to read and quiet that signal before it creates damage.
✅ 𝗘𝗮𝗿𝗹𝘆 𝘄𝗮𝗿𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘀𝘆𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗺 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗮𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗴
With over 300 million data points (!!!), their diagnostics can detect biological changes before symptoms show, like early signs of neurodegeneration or systemic inflammation.
✅ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗳𝗶𝗿𝘀𝘁 𝗱𝗶𝗮𝗴𝗻𝗼𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗰 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗮𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗲𝗱
Generation Lab measures how fast 19 organs are aging, well before disease or symptoms appear. It’s not sick care. It’s health management, driven by the speed of decline.
✅ 𝗛𝗲𝗮𝗹𝘁𝗵 𝗮𝘀 𝗮 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗴𝗿𝗮𝗺 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝗰𝗮𝗻 𝘂𝗽𝗴𝗿𝗮𝗱𝗲
Most people wait for symptoms to start. Generation Lab flips that model, treating aging itself as the earliest signal of decline, and giving people tools to act before they get sick.
One insight that stuck with me: Founders are aging 1.5x faster than average. The constant stress, travel, and sleep debt accelerates everything.
But they’re also the first to recognize the need to change course, and that urgency is fueling some of the most ambitious companies in health today.
[Generation Lab](https://www.linkedin.com/company/generation-lab-longevity?trk=public_post-text) is betting that we’ll be the last generation to age the way we do today.
♻️ Repost this if you're following the future of diagnostics and therapeutics. Follow me at [Delphine Le Grand](https://www.linkedin.com/in/delphinelegrandofficial?trk=public_post-text) for more.

![Josh Erndt-Marino, graphic]()

The Human Flourishing…•1K followers

Right now it’s the wild Wild West out here, and a lot of it isn’t making any sense to me. The first diagnostic for aging speed? The binary concept of diagnostic (yes / no) doesn’t really seem to mesh well with the underlying concept of speed (a continuous number with a wide range). I don’t mean to take anything away from the amazing progress and branding, but I am left wondering what’s the end game here. How do we reconcile certainty of branding with the reality that the science of aging is so poorly understood and rife with uncertainties?

![Tasha Baird-Miller, graphic]()

Third Quarter•3K followers

[Delphine Le Grand](https://www.linkedin.com/in/delphinelegrandofficial?trk=public_post_comment-text) This is the kind of innovation that shifts the paradigm — from treating symptoms to understanding the pace of decline before it starts. The idea that aging is software, not fate, is both empowering and urgent. Generation Lab isn’t just extending life — it’s redefining what it means to live well in the time we have.

![Alina Rui Su, graphic]()

Generation Lab•8K followers

The countdown is real.
If you are about 35 years old - you have 1751 weeks left for your lifetime.
Thats the reason we built [Generation Lab](https://www.linkedin.com/company/generation-lab-longevity?trk=public_post_comment-text) to Push Pause on Aging before the symptoms shows. Empower people to make the change about their own health.
Live Younger, Healthier, Longer.

## More Relevant Posts

![View profile for Delphine Le Grand]()

Eudēmonia Summit•28K followers

[CB Insights](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cb-insights?trk=public_post-text) just dropped the Tech Longevity Market Map.
A few years ago, “longevity” felt like science fiction, cryo chambers, billionaire biohackers, and labs promising immortality. Great stuff for the highlight reels, but not exactly moving the needle on actual health outcomes.
But now? It’s becoming one of the most dynamic sectors in health.
→ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲 𝗯𝗲𝘁𝘄𝗲𝗲𝗻 𝗵𝗲𝗮𝗹𝘁𝗵𝗰𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗹𝗼𝗻𝗴𝗲𝘃𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗶𝘀 𝗱𝗶𝘀𝗮𝗽𝗽𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴
What started as a fringe movement is turning into a full-scale ecosystem. The new market map highlights 84 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗮𝗻𝗶𝗲𝘀 𝗮𝗰𝗿𝗼𝘀𝘀 8 𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝗼𝗿𝗶𝗲𝘀, from cellular reprogramming and stem-cell preservation to early disease screening and hormone optimization.
The common thread? A shift from treating disease to extending healthspan.
→ 𝗣𝗿𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗰𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗶𝘀 𝘀𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗳𝗮𝘀𝘁
According to [CB Insights](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cb-insights?trk=public_post-text), longevity clinics increased 37% headcount growth yoy. This is pretty significant, and a sign of how quickly the space is expanding as consumer demand accelerates. A few examples, Sweden-based [Neko Health](https://se.linkedin.com/company/nekohealth?trk=public_post-text), co-founded by Spotify’s Daniel Ek, now has a 100 K+ waitlist for its full-body scans. [Function Health](https://www.linkedin.com/company/functionhealth?trk=public_post-text) acquired [Ezra](https://www.linkedin.com/company/ezrainc?trk=public_post-text) to combine blood testing with whole-body MRI. And [Midi Health](https://www.linkedin.com/company/midi-health?trk=public_post-text), which launched its longevity program in May, is on track for $150 M in 2025 revenue.
Membership-based clinics offering diagnostics, data tracking, and personalized guidance are rewriting what primary care looks like.
→ 𝗜𝗻𝘃𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘀 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗯𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗯𝗶𝗴 𝗼𝗻 𝗰𝗲𝗹𝗹𝘂𝗹𝗮𝗿 𝗿𝗲𝗷𝘂𝘃𝗲𝗻𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻
Now a bigger story is starting to emerge... the industrialization of longevity. We're seeing that biotech players like [Altos Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/altoslabs?trk=public_post-text), [Retro Biosciences](https://www.linkedin.com/company/retro-biosciences?trk=public_post-text), and [NewLimit](https://www.linkedin.com/company/newlimit?trk=public_post-text) are reframing aging as a solvable "systems problem", where biology can be optimized with the precision of software. Tech applied to medicine kinda mindset.

Building in Longevity

[CB Insights](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cb-insights?trk=public_post_reshare-text) just dropped the Tech Longevity Market Map.
A few years ago, “longevity” felt like science fiction, cryo chambers, billionaire biohackers, and labs promising immortality. Great stuff for the highlight reels, but not exactly moving the needle on actual health outcomes.
But now? It’s becoming one of the most dynamic sectors in health.
→ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲 𝗯𝗲𝘁𝘄𝗲𝗲𝗻 𝗵𝗲𝗮𝗹𝘁𝗵𝗰𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗹𝗼𝗻𝗴𝗲𝘃𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗶𝘀 𝗱𝗶𝘀𝗮𝗽𝗽𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴

+250 EXERCISE / aging postponed ... or indefinite life?
Even in the last 50 years, aging has changed radically. By 2276, it will be unrecognizable. Some prospective advances include:
CELLULAR & GENETIC REPROGRAMMING / Techniques like Yamanaka-factor reprogramming (resetting adult cells to a younger state) are already being tested in labs. In 250 years, this could allow continuous “resetting” of tissues, keeping biological age near zero indefinitely.
NANOMEDICINE & AI-DRIVEN REPAIR / Molecular-scale machines or AI-guided cellular editing could monitor and repair DNA, proteins, and organelles in real time, preventing the accumulation of age-related damage.
REPLACEMENT & REGENERATION / Bioprinted organs, neural tissue regeneration, even whole-body rejuvenation cycles may be routine. Instead of “repairing” indefinitely, people might schedule rejuvenation sessions every decade.
EPIGENETIC & METABOLIC CONTROL / Full mastery over our epigenome and metabolism likely means that aging could be toggled, slowed, or reversed much like adjusting a biological setting.
Huge questions arise, of course. One is equity. Will all this be available only to a few? Maybe not. By +250, the phase in which all these capabilities are rare and expensive might have passed. AI-driven automation at scale could drive costs down to the point where a complete rejuvenation is less expensive than a single hospital visit. So, staying young might be paid for by insurers, governments, public health entities, etc. because it is simply cheaper than treatment.
Another question: is "not aging" the same as "living forever"? Probably not, for a number of reasons. Accidents, suicides, subtle accumulated errors, perhaps neurological ... or maybe after a while most people will just get tired. A better term might be "living indefinitely" ...

Ten years ago, few believed people would pay out of pocket for continuous glucose monitors. Today, tracking data is standard for managing chronic conditions. It transformed diabetes care and gave patients control over their health.
 
Mental health still lacks that kind of objective, real-time data. Yet depression, pain, and mood all stem from neural activity. If we can record these signals, we can better understand and treat them.
 
Paradromics is developing the Connexus® Brain-Computer Interface, a fully implantable device built to continuously record detailed neural activity. It is a step toward a future with real-time neural biomarkers for mental health.
 
When the data exists, care can evolve. Imagine managing mental health by reading neural activity directly and adjusting care as needed.

Vocal biomarkers: Algorithms that analyze a person's voice to detect diseases are emerging.
Vocal biomarkers are specific features and signals within a person's voice that can indicate a health condition, acting as a signature for diseases like Parkinson's, respiratory disorders, and mental health issues. These biomarkers, analyzed using artificial intelligence and machine learning, provide remote health insights by detecting changes in acoustic properties (like pitch and pauses) and linguistic patterns (like fluency), offering potential for early diagnosis, monitoring disease progression, and even creating surrogate endpoints for clinical trials.

On today’s episode of Giant Ideas, I had the honour of speaking with Professor Bob Langer! Often called the 🌟Edison of Science🌟, he is one of the most cited researchers in history and a true pioneer in science and medicine.
As Co-Founder of [Moderna](https://www.linkedin.com/company/modernatx?trk=public_post-text), Dr Langer helped discover the revolutionary mRNA technology behind the COVID-19 vaccine, which played a pivotal role in ending the pandemic. His work has impacted over 2 billion lives— from cancer burns to heart disease and spinal injuries—even before Moderna’s breakthrough.
Here’s what we explored in our conversation:
📚 His Playbook: How to successfully bring science out of the lab and into the world.
❤️ Follow Your Passions: Why pursuing what you love (even if it’s not the highest-paying job) can change your life.
🪨 Rejection as a Gift: How Dr. Langer faced "failure" and rejection—including his 9 grant rejections—and persevered to revolutionize medicine.
🔑 Patents and Innovation: The importance of protecting your work to attract investment and scale impact.
🧠 American science and brain-on-chip technology: Why he believes science is thriving in the U.S. and he's bullish on neurotech.
Dr. Langer’s career is a masterclass in resilience, creativity, and the intersection of disciplines. His belief that breakthroughs often happen at the crossroads of seemingly unrelated fields is an inspiring reminder to think differently.
🎙️ Tune into this inspiring episode of Giant Ideas to learn from one of the greatest innovators of our time and a man who had 700 people show up to celebrate his 70th birthday! Listen to the full episode in the comments.

What happens when the world's #1 research nation decides to stop investing in its future?
We're about to find out
Nobel Prize officials just issued a stark warning: deep cuts to US research funding threaten to extinguish America’s long-held position as the world's scientific leader.
This goes far beyond politics. This is about the very engine of HealthTech innovation.
Consider the ripple effect:
- $9.5 billion in NIH grants, terminated.
- Projects for Alzheimer's and cancer, shelved.
- A potential 40% budget cut to the NIH.
- 75% of US scientists now consider leaving the country.
When the public sector retreats, the private sector must advance with new conviction.
This moment creates a profound responsibility for HealthTech leaders. We can no longer rely solely on the foundational research that has fueled decades of progress.
We must become the new patrons of discovery.
This means:
- Directly funding academic research with a long-term vision.
- Forging global partnerships to access the best minds, everywhere.
- Doubling down on R&D to translate the science that remains into tangible patient outcomes.
The landscape is shifting. China and Europe see this vacuum as their opportunity.
They are investing "unbelievable resources."
The question for us is simple: Will we watch the flame of American scientific leadership dim, or will we build new, resilient systems to fuel it ourselves?
The future of health innovation depends on our answer.

Last week at the [London Longevity Network](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/london-longevity?trk=public_post-text), [Jake P. Taylor-King](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/jptk?trk=public_post-text) (Cofounder [Relation](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/relation-therapeutics?trk=public_post-text)) gave a reality check on the state of longevity in the panel titled "Accelerating Longevity from Code to Cities".
Key takeaways:
1️⃣ Most longevity companies don’t yet have a clear FDA path but smart strategies are emerging (e.g. [Loyal](https://www.linkedin.com/company/loyalfordogs?trk=public_post-text)).
2️⃣ Polypharmacology > single interventions which often wreck mouse immune systems and don’t translate to humans. Ageing is too complex for one drug.
3️⃣ We are in the hype cycle. Products are outpacing evidence. But the field is maturing moving to real disease targets.
4️⃣ Epigenetic clocks show correlation but not endpoints; consumer tests often waste money.
5️⃣ Promising areas: Mitochondrial interventions are showing potential, single-cell eQTL studies are mapping genetic–phenotype relationships, and stem cell niche research is advancing across organ systems.

Recent PhD graduate Adebanjo Adedoja’s work on mitochondrial function reveals how mild stress to the cell’s powerhouses may unlock new ways to slow aging and improve health. Guided by his advisor, Associate Professor Alex Soukas at [Harvard Medical School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-medical-school/?trk=public_post-text), Adedoja found that tweaking mitochondrial regulatory pathways could potentially help protect against age-related diseases like Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, heart disease, and cancer.
What struck him most during his research was how the everyday lives around us give meaning to the science one pursues. It’s the choice to show up—for the people, for the community, for humility—that shapes how science can serve humanity.
Adedoja’s work reminds us that innovations don’t exist in a vacuum. They’re born from compassion, connection, and the courage to ask hard questions.

The Academy for Health & Lifespan Research is proud to be a member of [Lifespan Research Institute](https://www.linkedin.com/company/lifespanresearchinstitute?trk=public_post-text)’s Lifespan Alliance, and to support their latest initiative, the [Public Longevity Group](https://www.linkedin.com/company/public-longevity-group?trk=public_post-text) campaign.
Our contribution is helping launch the first public sentiment analysis engine for longevity science, building the cultural foundation for longer, healthier lives.
The Public Longevity Group (PLG) is addressing one of the biggest challenges in aging science—public skepticism. While longevity research is advancing rapidly, cultural resistance continues to slow progress and limit access to emerging therapies. PLG is developing the first AI-driven cultural intelligence system designed to understand and improve public trust in longevity.
This data-based approach will track real-time sentiment, identify which messages resonate or backfire, and guide how the field communicates with the public, policymakers, and investors.
Their campaign will fund tools like the Longevity Cultural Clock and a public sentiment dashboard, helping the longevity community translate scientific breakthroughs into broad public support. By aligning culture with science, PLG aims to ensure that longer, healthier lives become both achievable and widely embraced.

“This may work in India but it will never scale in a first-world country. Never”
A verbatim quote I had the pleasure of receiving from an extremely senior academic researcher at the Early Detection Conference (EDx) organized by Cancer Research UK, Stanford’s Canary Center, and OHSU that concluded this Thursday.
The context? A minute long conversation where I shared our latest data, with an unprecedented 87% sensitivity at Stage-1 and AUC of 0.962 for multiple cancers from our large 3000+ ppt study.
The researcher did not dispute the science or validity of our data or offer any reasoning on why our methodology wouldn’t scale. It was a fascinating window into a complete absence of first-principle thinking and open-mindedness.
It was also a good reminder that builders from “third-world countries” will not win by seeking approval and DEI scraps from the first-world-academia complex. We will only win by building technology and products that so thoroughly outclass the status-quo that there will be simply no choice but to adopt at scale.
The best part? We have an incredible arbitrage in talent and data, the most critical ingredients to building first-in-class and best-in-class healthcare products. With the right capital and culture, the third-world can bring-forth a whole-world where we can all live radically longer and healthier lives.
So are we building?

# **SCET Alum and Berkeley Professor Launch Generation Lab for Personalized Anti-Aging**

[![Berkeley Bioengineering Prof. Irina Conboy, Michael Suswal and SCET Alum and former Collider Cup winner, Alina Su](https://scet.berkeley.edu/wp-content/uploads/DSC02801-300x200.jpg)](https://scet.berkeley.edu/scet-alum-and-berkeley-professor-launch-generation-lab-for-personalized-anti-aging/ "SCET Alum and Berkeley Professor Launch Generation Lab for Personalized Anti-Aging")

**SCET Alum and [former Collider Cup](https://scet.berkeley.edu/collider-cup-profile-alina-su-ceo-founder-of-novaxs-biotech-corp-winner-of-pad-13-pitch-competition-member-of-berkeley-skydeck-program-and-1st-place-of-berkeley-fall-2021-collider-cup/) winner, Alina Su, and University of California, Berkeley Bioengineering Professor Irina Conboy are co-founding Generation Lab with the** **Mission to Extend the Human Healthspan – Waitlist for Its Clinically Driven At-Home Aging Test + Personalized Aging Intervention Opens Today**

[Generation Lab](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=TeZUXWpUv-2B6TCY38pVLo9nqCzlsziu-2F4wh5D2NlmVo9uL4RE6RyHrpPEhkFUxj8rHV6T_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioB280ksQbNBb8yCQth9i-2BjwifrcC0BGg1m5MgDd79GDbPPPLvnZ0Si1pKTGAmeTR4YNnjb7pC2XPr9vrCpxx74g41iDhTDaASDIJ58tCxW-2B8PR-2F0s1UKP7vYzbLO46-2FWZDR1hup1PFZ-2BrxMhh8XLxI-3D) will offer the first test based on biomedical evidence that measures an individual’s biological age progression, officially launched today out of stealth. The company is announcing its pre-seed funding and the opening of its [test kit waitlist](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=CVi7es-2FCNK-2FU4f58lHsDTlDj1TlDMlIK48xsd3z0C-2BA-3DZoqj_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4Joiuwcyerl-2FsPn4Y6mPgzeyYHHmKvmQ7w0dDw-2B3zOdFAPJHnr9b7kUXavh7Sp2xwWAmEd0CR9OZgw0fX7HOd7TcYf0-2BlAM9tMwGgVJQ94iTVwjJTFV-2B1hFCrJ0eHwKfZMK7MasE-2F9pLVZplCiGQhkNuy4-3D).

Co-founded by [Irina Conboy](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUU2LHKSbPg854wTzRTSv0e9PJ582xD6DNSESk2gn6QZaexS60B0sE2Zi9kyeK2jSBQ-3D-3DBHg5_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioJD040nHobNbpWz3V5eSPxVO0LZnsm0kHydeleYuGZWPAqLGD-2FWNnuMi78Y3hx-2FJJs7syIuUdGHZgTzLq0IeYHUkTq3kHAojLc41S7LnQF6xFlpjM1CvwfSNSR66Uv5gq7prLU1SDZd7kqOHJAnDKs-3D), a preeminent aging and longevity researcher from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines a simple cheek-swab test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions, and Generation Lab can measure the effectiveness of these over time.

According to the [CDC](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATURHuYuDkY-2F8kFuqzju5-2BdQf-2BplA0M-2BpvnD5M2MUQMvg8jjSButV23RpULQLJ66ySbw-3D-3DedM0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioyR5HW0MF-2F2k3wNtm38leZlFS8yrqfiu3ULrrxw8OXqO-2B-2FZRj7Zcq7E8zOrI9yqVhDXhwF7MbNUczj9zCLnhvMv0yj1ULZBvLvu2NQFYWgL-2B36K94LDfNISp4AuDz5ggr5YCIh28H2vxG1TnuAhq6M-3D), six in ten adults in the US have at least one chronic disease and four in ten adults have two or more. Many of these are correlated with aging. These diseases are a major cause of morbidity and mortality and place a significant burden on our healthcare system. Catching these conditions early and preventing their progression can save millions of lives and billions of dollars. That’s what Generation Lab plans to do.

Unlike biological clocks, which are based on linear models that predict aging and disease by comparing a few people to each other, Generation Lab quantifies the actual biological age of each person through precise measurement of molecular biological noise – a key barometer of aging. Moreover, the biological age is quantified by Generation Lab for each function of the body: inflammation, regeneration, homeostasis, reproduction, etc.  Generation Lab’s solution is based on peer-reviewed science – including groundbreaking research led by Dr. Irina Conboy, UC Berkeley, and published as a cover story in Aging in Sept 2023.

“Certain molecular disbalance causes degenerative diseases that flare up with aging,” explained Dr. Conboy, co-founder of Generation Lab, and Professor at University of California at Berkeley. “Generation Lab’s tests enable us to identify when a person has a proclivity towards certain conditions and suggest approaches that can help to attenuate, delay or even eliminate those risks. This paves the way to novel anti-aging medicine for identifying and treating diseases early – even when a person is pre-symptomatic – which leads to better outcomes.”

[George Church, Ph. D](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUdXTV3iQG28JGooOhy7cWzK92SocBokvhLFK6sB33ZQgpoi7p8Qlun30qw4VYNguOZoVNWv6gPcBqcnbfXxbMRQ-3DYiY0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4Joig4aeR72jOsxLtXKgAH76cI7dD1E-2FZo0r1b6sRHAf2grux18hcZ2XVWT9FthMeqfDyOGrDFUD7fMo6e2TnIKHNV45JCbtvf99DMKv9tdHE2W-2BAX5jlWzc6OiyDQjW-2FqpygTXEBo0Zo4y1TxIb-2BuBq4A-3D)., the Robert Winthrop Professor of Genetics at Harvard Medical School, is a renowned geneticist who is serving as an advisor to Generation Lab. “Irina and her team are taking cutting edge research straight out of the lab and applying it to a pressing issue: identifying disease much earlier than ever before possible using genetic markers,” said Dr. Church. “Seeing this unfold, and having a front-row seat to its impact, is going to be a pivotal moment in epigenetics – it’s incredibly exciting.”

Here’s how Generation Lab works:

* Obtain a test kit from a physician, clinic, or from Generation Lab directly.
* Open the kit and scan the QR code, selecting your level of membership (entry-level subscriptions start at about $400 per year for three tests).
* Follow the directions for a cheek swab and mail the sample back to Generation Lab in a pre-addressed envelope or return it to the clinician who provided the test.
* Within a few weeks, Generation Lab will provide a personalized report highlighting the status of your specific molecular aging markers, and offering a virtual call with a physician to review clinical and lifestyle interventions you might consider to slow the aging process or improve health.
* After subsequent Generation Lab tests, your clinician can review how effective each intervention was and its impact on your health and rate of aging.

Generation Lab’s other two co-founders are [Alina Rui Su](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUegha2L32XA5BGAQD-2FrrWT8ik1GwoEXnvGpGYc2nrM18TU4N_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoisM8XpuULqO1EOlO7whnQKgDfuutkYV8iNDIuuXaaNxIeNiGySaHts-2BOY8mibNTnXhhYJCWb7WQnV3eicUCcIvQwY3p7ss-2FZQ-2Ba24xxu0LyMjFwIUhcJgZbik2E27w-2BRVSK-2B1hm-2FXk2f5LggQRB-2Fmco-3D) and [Michael Suswal](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUegha2L32XA5BGAQD-2FrrWT-2FuMsN-2FvBofucFSDTFfKWPfrn49-2FbNt8DeQtyIvnpeQDQ-3D-3DOLIN_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoiiOiLgj4laIyYQqUlrv6DFacKhLUkeM5u-2F-2BpJ1fm5-2FlekiR26YXUQZrwohjCU31Bd0GNhCRJ1X4Pd7PXaPLnjpaPStkqp-2Bpu0PstT0ZkUWQtWMGyl3OH-2BHbDGn0iF4UWBKpAogFdrSujYkO3lSc-2FmRQ-3D). Su, who studied longevity at Harvard Medical School and Berkeley, was previously founder and CEO of NovaXS. She serves as Generation Lab’s CEO, setting the vision and leading the company’s development. Suswal, who was previously co-founder and COO of tech startup Standard AI (where he remains on the Board), serves as COO, leading Generation Lab’s operations and commercialization efforts.

“As rejuvenation research expands, we need to focus on identifying the most effective measurement for aging. My passion in healthcare led me to focus on Aging & Regeneration since I was a researcher in Irina’s lab at Berkeley,” said Su. “Generation Lab’s technology has the potential to impact an 8-billion-person global population. We will be a one-stop shop for testing plus clinician recommendations for interventions with the ability to ship personalized products to your doorstep. I’m proud that we’re making it easy for people to take a more proactive role in their healthcare and better understand their risk of aging factors.”

Generation Labs has raised a pre-seed round from Transpose Platform and Sequoia China Founding Partner. The company expects its aging test kits to be available in Q1 2024. To join the waitlist, visit [generationlab.co](http://generationlab.co/).

**About Generation Lab**

Co-founded by [Irina Conboy](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUU2LHKSbPg854wTzRTSv0e9PJ582xD6DNSESk2gn6QZaexS60B0sE2Zi9kyeK2jSBQ-3D-3DBHg5_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioJD040nHobNbpWz3V5eSPxVO0LZnsm0kHydeleYuGZWPAqLGD-2FWNnuMi78Y3hx-2FJJs7syIuUdGHZgTzLq0IeYHUkTq3kHAojLc41S7LnQF6xFlpjM1CvwfSNSR66Uv5gq7prLU1SDZd7kqOHJAnDKs-3D), a preeminent aging and longevity researcher from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines a simple cheek-swab test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions, and Generation Lab can measure the effectiveness of these over time.

According to the [CDC](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATURHuYuDkY-2F8kFuqzju5-2BdQf-2BplA0M-2BpvnD5M2MUQMvg8jjSButV23RpULQLJ66ySbw-3D-3DedM0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioyR5HW0MF-2F2k3wNtm38leZlFS8yrqfiu3ULrrxw8OXqO-2B-2FZRj7Zcq7E8zOrI9yqVhDXhwF7MbNUczj9zCLnhvMv0yj1ULZBvLvu2NQFYWgL-2B36K94LDfNISp4AuDz5ggr5YCIh28H2vxG1TnuAhq6M-3D), six in ten adults in the US have at least one chronic disease and four in ten adults have two or more. Many of these are correlated with aging. These diseases are a major cause of morbidity and mortality and place a significant burden on our healthcare system. Catching these conditions early and preventing their progression can save millions of lives and billions of dollars. That’s what Generation Lab plans to do.

Unlike biological clocks, which are based on linear models that predict aging and disease by comparing a few people to each other, Generation Lab quantifies the actual biological age of each person through precise measurement of molecular biological noise – a key barometer of aging. Moreover, the biological age is quantified by Generation Lab for each function of the body: inflammation, regeneration, homeostasis, reproduction, etc.  Generation Lab’s solution is based on peer-reviewed science – including groundbreaking research led by Dr. Irina Conboy, UC Berkeley, and published as a cover story in Aging in Sept 2023.

“Certain molecular disbalance causes degenerative diseases that flare up with aging,” explained Dr. Conboy, co-founder of Generation Lab, and Professor at University of California at Berkeley. “Generation Lab’s tests enable us to identify when a person has a proclivity towards certain conditions and suggest approaches that can help to attenuate, delay or even eliminate those risks. This paves the way to novel anti-aging medicine for identifying and treating diseases early – even when a person is pre-symptomatic – which leads to better outcomes.”

Su, who studied longevity at Harvard Medical School and Berkeley, was previously founder and CEO of NovaXS. She serves as Generation Lab’s CEO, setting the vision and leading the company’s development. Suswal, who was previously co-founder and COO of tech startup Standard AI (where he remains on the Board), serves as COO, leading Generation Lab’s operations and commercialization efforts.

“As rejuvenation research expands, we need to focus on identifying the most effective measurement for aging. My passion in healthcare led me to focus on Aging & Regeneration since I was a researcher in Irina’s lab at Berkeley,” said Su. “Generation Lab’s technology has the potential to impact an 8-billion-person global population. We will be a one-stop shop for testing plus clinician recommendations for interventions with the ability to ship personalized products to your doorstep. I’m proud that we’re making it easy for people to take a more proactive role in their healthcare and better understand their risk of aging factors.”

Generation Labs has raised a pre-seed round from Transpose Platform and Sequoia China Founding Partner. The company expects its aging test kits to be available in Q1 2024. To join the waitlist, visit [generationlab.co](http://generationlab.co/).

According to the [CDC](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATURHuYuDkY-2F8kFuqzju5-2BdQf-2BplA0M-2BpvnD5M2MUQMvg8jjSButV23RpULQLJ66ySbw-3D-3DedM0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioyR5HW0MF-2F2k3wNtm38leZlFS8yrqfiu3ULrrxw8OXqO-2B-2FZRj7Zcq7E8zOrI9yqVhDXhwF7MbNUczj9zCLnhvMv0yj1ULZBvLvu2NQFYWgL-2B36K94LDfNISp4AuDz5ggr5YCIh28H2vxG1TnuAhq6M-3D), six in ten adults in the US have at least one chronic disease and four in ten adults have two or more. Many of these are correlated with aging. These diseases are a major cause of morbidity and mortality and place a significant burden on our healthcare system. Catching these conditions early and preventing their progression can save millions of lives and billions of dollars. That’s what Generation Lab plans to do.

Unlike biological clocks, which are based on linear models that predict aging and disease by comparing a few people to each other, Generation Lab quantifies the actual biological age of each person through precise measurement of molecular biological noise – a key barometer of aging. Moreover, the biological age is quantified by Generation Lab for each function of the body: inflammation, regeneration, homeostasis, reproduction, etc.  Generation Lab’s solution is based on peer-reviewed science – including groundbreaking research led by Dr. Irina Conboy, UC Berkeley, and published as a cover story in Aging in Sept 2023.

“Certain molecular disbalance causes degenerative diseases that flare up with aging,” explained Dr. Conboy, co-founder of Generation Lab, and Professor at University of California at Berkeley. “Generation Lab’s tests enable us to identify when a person has a proclivity towards certain conditions and suggest approaches that can help to attenuate, delay or even eliminate those risks. This paves the way to novel anti-aging medicine for identifying and treating diseases early – even when a person is pre-symptomatic – which leads to better outcomes.”

Here’s how Generation Lab works:

* Obtain a test kit from a physician, clinic, or from Generation Lab directly.
* Open the kit and scan the QR code, selecting your level of membership (entry-level subscriptions start at about $400 per year for three tests).
* Follow the directions for a cheek swab and mail the sample back to Generation Lab in a pre-addressed envelope or return it to the clinician who provided the test.
* Within a few weeks, Generation Lab will provide a personalized report highlighting the status of your specific molecular aging markers, and offering a virtual call with a physician to review clinical and lifestyle interventions you might consider to slow the aging process or improve health.
* After subsequent Generation Lab tests, your clinician can review how effective each intervention was and its impact on your health and rate of aging.

Generation Labs has raised a pre-seed round from Transpose Platform and Sequoia China Founding Partner. The company expects its aging test kits to be available in Q1 2024. To join the waitlist, visit [generationlab.co](http://generationlab.co/).
